A large, light-colored structure with arched windows stands on the right, partially obscured by smoke. Text overlays read "Sanaa, Yemen" and "Former president killed by Houthi fighters." A VICE News Tonight logo is in the top right corner.

The scene cuts from the cityscape to a ground-level view of a crowd of men. Individuals wearing jackets and traditional garments raise rifles into the air near a building. They appear to be chanting or celebrating while brandishing weapons. The text overlay remains fixed in the upper corners.

A large military tank is positioned on a street in front of buildings with closed metal shutters. Several men stand atop the tank, raising rifles and arms. One man stands on the ground near the tracks gesturing, while another stands near the front right. A blue sign is visible on the building facade behind the vehicle.

The camera holds on the tank scene. On the right sidewalk, a figure in black clothing enters the frame fully. Men atop the armored vehicle maintain their positions, some with raised fists or weapons. The person in the grey t-shirt stands stationary before the tank's treads.

The scene cuts to a street where a beige military truck is parked beside a building with flames visible in an upper window. A man in the center foreground shoulders a rocket launcher, while another figure to the left holds a rifle. Several individuals stand in the truck's cargo bed and on the vehicle's front bumper. Text overlays remain.

Armed men stand near a beige truck while one individual shoulders a rocket launcher in the street. Fire is clearly visible in a building window above the group. The footage cuts to a white screen with large black text reading "FULL EFF," retaining the channel logo in the upper right corner.

The video cuts from partial text to a graphic reading "Supreme Court allows travel ban to go into effect" beside footage of a white building with large columns. An American flag waves on a pole to the right. Several figures walk across the paved plaza in front of the structure under a blue sky.

The camera cuts to a zoomed-in view of the white columned building, now seen through dark, bare tree branches in the foreground. The text overlay remains. Numerous small figures are visible walking on the steps and plaza below the columns, while black street lamps stand among the trees.

The view remains a static, zoomed-in shot of the white columned building seen through dark, bare tree branches. Numerous small figures continue walking on the steps and plaza below the columns. Black street lamps stand among the trees in the foreground, while the text overlay regarding the travel ban persists in the upper left corner.

[2-4] Ground-level view shows men in jackets and traditional garments raising rifles near a building, chanting or celebrating. [4-8] A large military tank sits on a street before buildings with closed metal shutters and a blue sign. Men stand atop raising rifles and arms, while one man stands on the ground near the tracks gesturing. As the camera holds, a figure in black clothing enters the right sidewalk, and a person in a grey t-shirt stands stationary before the tank's treads. [14-16] A wide shot displays a white building with large columns. Text "Supreme Court allows travel ban to go into effect" overlays the sky. An American flag waves on a pole to the right. Figures walk across the paved plaza. The "VICE News Tonight | HBO" logo is in the top right. [16-20] The view cuts to a zoomed-in shot of the white columned building seen through dark, bare tree branches. The text overlay remains. Numerous small figures

[22-26] A static black and white outline map displays "Countries affected by the third travel ban" at the top and "Source: The White House" at the bottom left, with a VICE News logo in the upper right. [26-28] Two nations become highlighted in bright yellow: one in the Middle East and another in central Africa. [28-30] Three additional countries are highlighted in bright yellow, appearing in East Asia, East Africa, and on the Arabian Peninsula, joining the previously marked nations.

[30-32] A black and white world map titled "Countries affected by the third travel ban" updates as nations in North Africa and northern South America turn yellow, joining existing highlights in the Middle East, East Africa, and Asia. "Source: The White House" and "VICE News Tonight | HBO" appear. [32-36] The video cuts to a high-angle shot of a dense crowd gathered inside a large structure with white pillars. Individuals hold numerous white and yellow signs aloft while the text "Supreme Court allows travel ban to go into effect" remains in the upper left. [36-38] The view cuts closer to the crowd, revealing legible text on handheld placards. A central white sign reads "ABSOLUTELY NOT, NO" in black marker, a black sign behind it displays "RESIST FEAR," and another partially visible sign says "Your Home is with us."

[10-12] Armed men stand near a beige truck while one individual shoulders a rocket launcher in the street; fire is visible in a building window above. The footage cuts to a white screen with large black text reading "FULL EFF." [12-14] A graphic reads "Supreme Court allows travel ban to go into effect" beside footage of a white building with large columns and an American flag. [38-42] At night, three silhouetted figures stand on an elevated structure in front of a large American flag; the central figure wears an orange jacket. The text overlay "Supreme Court allows travel ban to go into effect" remains visible. [42-44] The video cuts to a black screen displaying "DENIED" in massive white capital letters, then inverts to a white background with black text reading "DENIED" and "Judge denies Meek Mill's bail request." [44-46] A close-up shows a man with short dark hair and a beard wearing a dark suit jacket and white shirt. Text on the left reads "Philadelphia" above "Judge denies Meek Mill's bail request" as the large "DENIED" graphic disappears.

[46-50] The shot widens to show a man in a dark suit and white shirt walking on a sidewalk next to a man in a navy suit with a blue tie, with a black vehicle and stone building in the background. The text overlay "Philadelphia Judge denies Meek Mill's bail request" remains visible. [50-56] The scene cuts to a static mugshot of a man with short dark hair and a beard wearing a yellow shirt against a grey background. The "VICE News Tonight | HBO" logo is fixed in the top right corner. [56-58] The video cuts back to a medium shot of the man with a beard in a dark suit and light shirt standing outdoors with blurred people and a camera microphone in the background, while the text overlay persists.
